Operation 'listApps' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Current description is too minimal and lacks context about what app data is returned, pagination support, and use cases. Enhanced description specifies the returned app object properties, pagination capability, and practical use cases to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation effectively.

data.current_description: Return a list of all Apps in a OneLogin account.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a list of all applications configured in a OneLogin account. Returns app objects containing configuration details such as app ID, name, icon URL, and authentication settings. Supports pagination through query parameters. Use this operation to discover available apps or retrieve app configurations for integration purposes.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal and lacks context about what app data is returned, pagination support, and use cases. Enhanced description specifies the returned app object properties, pagination capability, and practical use cases to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation effectively.

message: Operation 'generateToken' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description lacks important details about the response format, token usage, and expiration behavior. Enhanced description specifies the request requirements, complete response structure (token type, expiration, refresh token), how to use the token for authentication, and token lifecycle management. This enables AI agents to properly handle token generation, storage, and refresh workflows.

data.current_description: Generate an access token using client credentials (client_id and client_secret).
data.description_suggestion: Generate an OAuth 2.0 access token using client credentials authentication. Requires valid client_id and client_secret in the request body. Returns a TokenResponse object containing the access token, token type (bearer), expiration time in seconds, and refresh token if applicable. The generated token is used to authenticate subsequent API requests via the Authorization header. Tokens expire after the specified duration and must be regenerated for continued access.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks important details about the response format, token usage, and expiration behavior. Enhanced description specifies the request requirements, complete response structure (token type, expiration, refresh token), how to use the token for authentication, and token lifecycle management. This enables AI agents to properly handle token generation, storage, and refresh workflows.
